literal_meaning: when this is not, this is not
preferred_translation: when this is not, this does not come to be
definition: Negative conditionality formula for the non-arising side of dependent arising.
notes: Use this record for the whole negative formula line rather than rebuilding it from separate headwords. It belongs with the four standard conditionality lines and prepares the quenching line that follows. The Buddhadasa usage profile supports keeping this line in the same practical conditionality family.

context_rules.rendering: when this is not, this does not come to be

context_rules.context: When a more literal English line is needed
context_rules.rendering: when this is absent, this does not come to be

example_phrases.translation: when this is not, this does not come to be

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping the line in the practical conditionality family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard negative conditionality line.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not replace it with a quenching formula when the text is marking non-presence rather than nirodha explicitly.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the negative line from collapsing into a generic absence statement without formula discipline.
